rulings.Case Title: QUALITY INTERNATIONAL PACKAGING, A CORPORATION VS. WORLD OF CHARMS INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT WORLD OF CHARMS, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Overruled as to the causes of action for money due, book account, quantum meruit, declaratory relief, and fraudulent transfer. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately pleads facts to state those causes of action. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the fraud cause of action. The SAC does not plead fraud with the requisite specificity. In order to state a cause of action for fraud against a corporate defendant, the complaint must allege who at the corporation made such statements and allege their authority to speak for the corporation. (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Sustained without leave to amend as to the constructive trustee cause of action. "A constructive trust is not an independent cause of action but merely a type of remedy." (Batt v. City and County of San Francisco (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 65, 82.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE LEE CEARLOCK et al VS. ALYCE D. CRAFT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Alyce D. Crafts Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, With Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALYCE CRAFT'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, With Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Jeffrey Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party must bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. FATAI ADEWUSI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT FATAI ADEWUSI MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Defendant shall file its proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. "[T]he law strongly favors trial and disposition on the merits." (Elston v. Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233.) The Declaration of Fatai Adewusi establishes that Defendant did not know about the case until after his default was entered and therefore, Defendant is entitled to relief pursuant to CCP sec. 473.5. Defendant did not unreasonably delay in bringing this motion, as his declaration establishes that he attempted to settle with Plaintiff before filing this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YIU LUN WONG VS. HON WEI CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 10, 2015, Line 14, RESPONDENT HON CHAN Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C). Granted. Defendant Yiu Lun Wong shall pay Plaintiff Hon Wei Chan reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $24,913.50 and costs in the amount of $1,602.00 within 30 days of entry of this order, pursuant to Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c). The plain language of Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c) provides that an employee is the prevailing party if the court amounts an award greater than zero. Plaintiff recovered $6,074.53 upon Defendant dismissing the appeal, and therefore, Plaintiff is entitled to recover fees and costs. Equitable principles support this result. Because Defendant filed a timely notice of appeal, Plaintiff was obligated to expend attorney's fees and costs through the date of trial. It would be inequitable to require Plaintiff to bear the burden of these costs. Further, Arias v. Kardoulias (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1429, the only case cited by Defendant in opposition, is inapposite. There, the trial court never obtained jurisdiction over the matter because the appellant did not timely file a notice of appeal. (Id. at 1438.) The court in Arias expressly limited its holding, stating that it "express[es] no opinion on the case in which a timely appeal from a commissioner's decision is dismissed on other grounds." (Id. at 1439, fn. 5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
